_comment: ADK eval test case — Math Grade 4 VN. Tests that the fixed 17-section template generates coherently for a non-language-arts subject.

curriculum.competency_description: Compare fractions with unlike denominators using visual models and equivalent fractions.

sub_competencies.label: Use a common denominator to compare
Order fractions on a number line

instructional_design.core_concept: Two fractions with different denominators can be compared by rewriting them with a common denominator, allowing direct comparison of the numerators.
bloom_targets: Remember the meaning of numerator and denominator
Understand how to find the least common denominator
Apply equivalent fraction methods to compare and order fractions
instructional_design.essential_question_seed: How can you tell which of two fractions is larger when the bottom numbers are different?

assertions.description: All 17 sections must be present regardless of subject
All required section keys must exist in session state

assertions.name: vocab_presence
assertions.description: Math vocabulary (numerator, denominator, equivalent, etc.) must appear in body sections

assertions.name: self_assess_targets
assertions.description: Self-assessment skills must match learning targets verbatim

assertions.name: answer_key_quotes
assertions.description: Answer key must quote from assessment passage

assertions.name: passage_domain_diff
assertions.description: Assessment and model passage must use different topic domains
GenerateResponse must match the documented schema
